pattern: Tally how often each decimal digit appears, then break ties toward the smallest digit when picking the rarest one.
hints: Work over the string of digits, or repeatedly take n modulo 10 while dividing by 10.
Only digits that actually appear count; a digit with zero occurrences is not a candidate.
Track the minimum nonzero count, and among digits achieving it keep the numerically smallest digit.

edge_inputs.description: example with one unique rarest digit
several digits tie at frequency one, pick smallest
leading digit is largest but rarest is smaller

stress.description: random integers up to the 32-bit cap
